file.content:
.PHONY: help check-deps install-deps tree ln submodule install build clean rebuild test format update

# ---------------------------
# Build resource controls
# ---------------------------
# By default, build uses all available CPU cores, but users can override:
#   make build MAX_JOBS=2 CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=2 CMAKE_ARGS="-DSGL_KERNEL_COMPILE_THREADS=1"
NPROC ?= $(shell nproc 2>/dev/null || echo 1)
MAX_JOBS ?= $(NPROC)
CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL ?= $(MAX_JOBS)
UV_BUILD_DIR ?= build
CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM ?= 3.5

# Optional GitHub mirror for FetchContent (e.g. internal artifactory).
# Empty -> CMakeLists.txt default (github.com).
GITHUB_ARTIFACTORY ?=
ifneq ($(strip $(GITHUB_ARTIFACTORY)),)
CMAKE_ARGS += -DGITHUB_ARTIFACTORY=$(GITHUB_ARTIFACTORY)
endif

# Show help for each target
help: ## Show this help message
	@echo "Available targets:"
	@grep -E '^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+:.*?## .*$$' $(MAKEFILE_LIST) | sort | awk 'BEGIN {FS = ":.*?## "}; {printf "\033[36m%-20s\033[0m %s\n", $$1, $$2}'

check-deps: ## Check and install required Python formatting dependencies
	@command -v isort >/dev/null 2>&1 || (echo "Installing isort..." && pip install isort)
	@command -v black >/dev/null 2>&1 || (echo "Installing black..." && pip install black)

install-deps: ## Install Python formatting tools (isort and black)
	pip install scikit-build-core isort black

tree: ## Show project directory structure
	@tree --prune -I "__pycache__|*.egg-info|*.so|build|3rdparty|dist"

submodule: ## Initialize and update git submodules
	@git submodule update --init --recursive

ln: submodule ## Create compilation database
	@rm -rf build && mkdir build && cd build && cmake .. -DCMAKE_EXPORT_COMPILE_COMMANDS=YES -DCMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=3.5

install: submodule ## Install package in development mode
	@pip install -e . --no-build-isolation

build: install-deps submodule ## Build and install wheel package
	@rm -rf dist/* || true && \
		CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM=$(CMAKE_POLICY_VERSION_MINIMUM) \
		MAX_JOBS=$(MAX_JOBS) \
		CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL=$(CMAKE_BUILD_PARALLEL_LEVEL) \
		CMAKE_ARGS="$(CMAKE_ARGS)" \
		uv build --wheel -Cbuild-dir=$(UV_BUILD_DIR) . --verbose --color=always --no-build-isolation && \
		pip3 install dist/*whl --force-reinstall --no-deps

clean: ## Remove build artifacts
	@rm -rf build dist *.egg-info

rebuild: clean submodule build ## Clean and rebuild the project
	@echo "Succeed to rebuild"

test: ## Run all tests
	@find tests -name "test_*.py" | xargs -n 1 python3

format: check-deps ## Format all source files
	@echo "Formatting source files..."
	@find csrc tests -name '*.cc' -o -name '*.cu' -o -name '*.cuh' -o -name '*.h' -o -name '*.hpp' | xargs clang-format -i
	@find python tests -name '*.py' | xargs isort
	@find python tests -name '*.py' | xargs black
	@pre-commit run --all-files

FILES_TO_UPDATE = python/sgl_kernel/version.py \
                 pyproject.toml \
                 pyproject_rocm.toml \
                 pyproject_cpu.toml

update: ## Update version numbers across project files. Usage: make update <new_version>
	@if [ -z "$(filter-out $@,$(MAKECMDGOALS))" ]; then \
		echo "Version required. Usage: make update <new_version>"; \
		exit 1; \
	fi
	@OLD_VERSION=$$(grep "version" python/sgl_kernel/version.py | cut -d '"' -f2); \
	NEW_VERSION=$(filter-out $@,$(MAKECMDGOALS)); \
	echo "Updating version from $$OLD_VERSION to $$NEW_VERSION"; \
	for file in $(FILES_TO_UPDATE); do \
		if [ "$(shell uname)" = "Darwin" ]; then \
			sed -i '' -e "s/$$OLD_VERSION/$$NEW_VERSION/g" $$file; \
		else \
			sed -i -e "s/$$OLD_VERSION/$$NEW_VERSION/g" $$file; \
		fi \
	done; \
	echo "Version update complete"

%:
	@:

74	# These implementations extensively draw from and build upon the FlashInfer project https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer
75	# Kudos to @yzh119
76	def rmsnorm(
77	    input: torch.Tensor,
78	    weight: torch.Tensor,
79	    eps: float = 1e-6,
80	    out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
81	    enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,
82	) -> torch.Tensor:
83	    r"""Root mean square normalization.
84	
85	    ``out[i] = (input[i] / RMS(input)) * weight[i]``
86	
87	    Parameters
88	    ----------
89	    input: torch.Tensor
90	        Input tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
91	    weight: torch.Tensor
92	        Weight tensor, shape (hidden_size,).
93	    eps: float
94	        Epsilon for numerical stability.
95	    out: Optional[torch.Tensor]
96	        The output tensor, if specified, the kernel will update this tensor inplace.
97	    enable_pdl: Optional[bool]
98	        Whether to enable `programmatic dependent launch
99	        <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#programmatic-dependent-launch-and-synchronization>`_
100	        If None, will be automatically enabled on Hopper architecture.
101	
102	    Returns
103	    -------
104	    output: torch.Tensor
105	        Normalized tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
106	    """
107	    # torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling(): FlashInfer norm paths are not safe under
108	    # torch.compile(..., fullgraph=True). Dynamo traces into FlashInfer's JIT module
109	    # loading path, which calls Path.exists() / os.stat() — both untraceable — causing
110	    # the entire compilation to fail. We fall back to the internal implementation while
111	    # tracing as a temporary workaround. Once the upstream fix is merged and we upgrade
112	    # FlashInfer, this check can be removed.
113	    # See: https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/issues/2734
114	    #      https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/pull/2733
115	    if (
116	        _has_flashinfer
117	        and input.dtype in _FLASHINFER_NORM_SUPPORTED_DTYPES
118	        and not torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling()
119	    ):
120	        return _flashinfer_norm.rmsnorm(input, weight, eps, out, enable_pdl)
121	    else:
122	        return _rmsnorm_internal(input, weight, eps, out, enable_pdl)
123	
124	
125	def fused_add_rmsnorm(
126	    input: torch.Tensor,
127	    residual: torch.Tensor,
128	    weight: torch.Tensor,
129	    eps: float = 1e-6,
130	    enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,
131	) -> None:
132	    r"""Fused add root mean square normalization.
133	
134	    Step 1:
135	    ``residual[i] += input[i]``
136	
137	    Step 2:
138	    ``input[i] = (residual[i] / RMS(residual)) * weight[i]``
139	
140	    Parameters
141	    ----------
142	    input: torch.Tensor
143	        Input tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
144	    residual: torch.Tensor
145	        Residual tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
146	    weight: torch.Tensor
147	        Weight tensor, shape (hidden_size,).
148	    eps: float
149	        Epsilon for numerical stability.
150	    enable_pdl: Optional[bool]
151	        Whether to enable `programmatic dependent launch
152	        <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#programmatic-dependent-launch-and-synchronization>`_
153	        If None, will be automatically enabled on Hopper architecture.
154	    """
155	    if (
156	        _has_flashinfer
157	        and input.dtype in _FLASHINFER_NORM_SUPPORTED_DTYPES
158	        and not torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling()
159	    ):
160	        _flashinfer_norm.fused_add_rmsnorm(input, residual, weight, eps, enable_pdl)
161	    else:
162	        _fused_add_rmsnorm_internal(input, residual, weight, eps, enable_pdl)
163

165	def gemma_rmsnorm(
166	    input: torch.Tensor,
167	    weight: torch.Tensor,
168	    eps: float = 1e-6,
169	    out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
170	    enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,
171	) -> torch.Tensor:
172	    r"""Gemma-style root mean square normalization.
173	
174	    ``out[i] = (input[i] / RMS(input)) * (weight[i] + 1)``
175	
176	    Parameters
177	    ----------
178	    input: torch.Tensor
179	        Input tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
180	    weight: torch.Tensor
181	        Weight tensor, shape (hidden_size,).
182	    eps: float
183	        Epsilon for numerical stability.
184	    out: Optional[torch.Tensor]
185	        The output tensor, if specified, the kernel will update this tensor inplace.
186	    enable_pdl: Optional[bool]
187	        Whether to enable `programmatic dependent launch
188	        <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#programmatic-dependent-launch-and-synchronization>`_
189	        If None, will be automatically enabled on Hopper architecture.
190	
191	    Returns
192	    -------
193	    output: torch.Tensor
194	        Gemma Normalized tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
195	    """
196	    if (
197	        _has_flashinfer
198	        and input.dtype in _FLASHINFER_NORM_SUPPORTED_DTYPES
199	        and not torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling()
200	    ):
201	        return _flashinfer_norm.gemma_rmsnorm(input, weight, eps, out, enable_pdl)
202	    else:
203	        return _gemma_rmsnorm_internal(input, weight, eps, out, enable_pdl)

215	    Step 1:

220

248	def _check_shape(input: torch.Tensor, output: torch.Tensor) -> None:
249	    assert input.ndim == output.ndim, f"{input.ndim} != {output.ndim}"
250	    assert (
251	        input.shape[:-1] == output.shape[:-1]
252	    ), f"{input.shape[:-1]} != {output.shape[:-1]}"
253	    assert (
254	        input.shape[-1] == 2 * output.shape[-1]
255	    ), f"{input.shape[-1]} != {2 * output.shape[-1]}"
256

258	def silu_and_mul(input: torch.Tensor, out: torch.Tensor = None) -> torch.Tensor:
259	    if input.shape[-1] * input.dtype.itemsize % 16 != 0:
260	        raise ValueError("The pointers must be multiple of 16 bytes.")
261	    if out is not None:
262	        _check_shape(input, out)
263	    else:
264	        out = torch.empty(
265	            input.shape[:-1] + (input.shape[-1] // 2,),
266	            device=input.device,
267	            dtype=input.dtype,
268	        )
269	    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.silu_and_mul.default(out, input)
270	    return out

275	        raise ValueError("The pointers must be multiple of 16 bytes.")
276	    if out is not None:
277	        _check_shape(input, out)
278	    else:
279	        out = torch.empty(
280	            input.shape[:-1] + (input.shape[-1] // 2,),
281	            device=input.device,
282	            dtype=input.dtype,
283	        )

288	def gelu_and_mul(input: torch.Tensor, out: torch.Tensor = None) -> torch.Tensor:
289	    if input.shape[-1] * input.dtype.itemsize % 16 != 0:
290	        raise ValueError("The pointers must be multiple of 16 bytes.")
291	    if out is not None:
292	        _check_shape(input, out)
293	    else:
294	        out = torch.empty(
295	            input.shape[:-1] + (input.shape[-1] // 2,),

303	if torch.version.hip is not None:
304	
305	    def gelu_quick(input: torch.Tensor, out: torch.Tensor = None) -> torch.Tensor:
306	        """
307	        Quick-GELU:  y = x * sigmoid(1.702 * x)
308	
309	        The CUDA/HIP kernel uses 128-bit (16-byte) vector loads & stores,
310	        so the last-dimension byte length must be a multiple of 16 bytes.
311	        """
312	        if input.shape[-1] * input.dtype.itemsize % 16 != 0:
313	            raise ValueError(
314	                f"The last dimension ({input.shape[-1]}) x itemsize "
315	                f"({input.dtype.itemsize}) must be a multiple of 16 bytes."
316	            )
317	
318	        if out is not None:
319	            assert input.shape == out.shape, f"{input.shape} != {out.shape}"
320	        else:
321	            out = torch.empty_like(input)
322	
323	        torch.ops.sgl_kernel.gelu_quick(out, input)
324	        return out

from typing import Optional

import torch
from sgl_kernel.utils import is_arch_support_pdl

try:
    import flashinfer.norm as _flashinfer_norm

    _has_flashinfer = True
except ImportError:
    _has_flashinfer = False

_FLASHINFER_NORM_SUPPORTED_DTYPES = {torch.float16, torch.bfloat16}


def _rmsnorm_internal(
    input: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float,
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if out is None:
        out = torch.empty_like(input)
    if enable_pdl is None:
        enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.rmsnorm.default(out, input, weight, eps, enable_pdl)
    return out


def _fused_add_rmsnorm_internal(
    input: torch.Tensor,
    residual: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float,
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool],
) -> None:
    if enable_pdl is None:
        enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.fused_add_rmsnorm.default(
        input, residual, weight, eps, enable_pdl
    )


def _gemma_rmsnorm_internal(
    input: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float,
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor],
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool],
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if out is None:
        out = torch.empty_like(input)
    if enable_pdl is None:
        enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.gemma_rmsnorm.default(out, input, weight, eps, enable_pdl)
    return out


def _gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm_internal(
    input: torch.Tensor,
    residual: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float,
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool],
) -> None:
    if enable_pdl is None:
        enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm.default(
        input, residual, weight, eps, enable_pdl
    )


# These implementations extensively draw from and build upon the FlashInfer project https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer
# Kudos to @yzh119
def rmsnorm(
    input: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float = 1e-6,
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    r"""Root mean square normalization.

    ``out[i] = (input[i] / RMS(input)) * weight[i]``

    Parameters
    ----------
    input: torch.Tensor
        Input tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
    weight: torch.Tensor
        Weight tensor, shape (hidden_size,).
    eps: float
        Epsilon for numerical stability.
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor]
        The output tensor, if specified, the kernel will update this tensor inplace.
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool]
        Whether to enable `programmatic dependent launch
        <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#programmatic-dependent-launch-and-synchronization>`_
        If None, will be automatically enabled on Hopper architecture.

    Returns
    -------
    output: torch.Tensor
        Normalized tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
    """
    # torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling(): FlashInfer norm paths are not safe under
    # torch.compile(..., fullgraph=True). Dynamo traces into FlashInfer's JIT module
    # loading path, which calls Path.exists() / os.stat() — both untraceable — causing
    # the entire compilation to fail. We fall back to the internal implementation while
    # tracing as a temporary workaround. Once the upstream fix is merged and we upgrade
    # FlashInfer, this check can be removed.
    # See: https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/issues/2734
    #      https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/pull/2733
    if (
        _has_flashinfer
        and input.dtype in _FLASHINFER_NORM_SUPPORTED_DTYPES
        and not torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling()
    ):
        return _flashinfer_norm.rmsnorm(input, weight, eps, out, enable_pdl)
    else:
        return _rmsnorm_internal(input, weight, eps, out, enable_pdl)


def fused_add_rmsnorm(
    input: torch.Tensor,
    residual: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float = 1e-6,
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,
) -> None:
    r"""Fused add root mean square normalization.

    Step 1:
    ``residual[i] += input[i]``

    Step 2:
    ``input[i] = (residual[i] / RMS(residual)) * weight[i]``

    Parameters
    ----------
    input: torch.Tensor
        Input tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
    residual: torch.Tensor
        Residual tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
    weight: torch.Tensor
        Weight tensor, shape (hidden_size,).
    eps: float
        Epsilon for numerical stability.
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool]
        Whether to enable `programmatic dependent launch
        <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#programmatic-dependent-launch-and-synchronization>`_
        If None, will be automatically enabled on Hopper architecture.
    """
    if (
        _has_flashinfer
        and input.dtype in _FLASHINFER_NORM_SUPPORTED_DTYPES
        and not torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling()
    ):
        _flashinfer_norm.fused_add_rmsnorm(input, residual, weight, eps, enable_pdl)
    else:
        _fused_add_rmsnorm_internal(input, residual, weight, eps, enable_pdl)


def gemma_rmsnorm(
    input: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float = 1e-6,
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    r"""Gemma-style root mean square normalization.

    ``out[i] = (input[i] / RMS(input)) * (weight[i] + 1)``

    Parameters
    ----------
    input: torch.Tensor
        Input tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
    weight: torch.Tensor
        Weight tensor, shape (hidden_size,).
    eps: float
        Epsilon for numerical stability.
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor]
        The output tensor, if specified, the kernel will update this tensor inplace.
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool]
        Whether to enable `programmatic dependent launch
        <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#programmatic-dependent-launch-and-synchronization>`_
        If None, will be automatically enabled on Hopper architecture.

    Returns
    -------
    output: torch.Tensor
        Gemma Normalized tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
    """
    if (
        _has_flashinfer
        and input.dtype in _FLASHINFER_NORM_SUPPORTED_DTYPES
        and not torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling()
    ):
        return _flashinfer_norm.gemma_rmsnorm(input, weight, eps, out, enable_pdl)
    else:
        return _gemma_rmsnorm_internal(input, weight, eps, out, enable_pdl)


def gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm(
    input: torch.Tensor,
    residual: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float = 1e-6,
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,
) -> None:
    r"""Gemma-style fused add root mean square normalization.

    Step 1:
    ``residual[i] += input[i]``

    Step 2:
    ``input[i] = (residual[i] / RMS(residual)) * (weight + 1)``

    Parameters
    ----------
    input: torch.Tensor
        Input tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
    residual: torch.Tensor
        Residual tensor, shape (batch_size, hidden_size).
    weight: torch.Tensor
        Weight tensor, shape (hidden_size,).
    eps: float
        Epsilon for numerical stability.
    enable_pdl: Optional[bool]
        Whether to enable `programmatic dependent launch
        <https://docs.nvidia.com/cuda/cuda-c-programming-guide/index.html#programmatic-dependent-launch-and-synchronization>`_
        If None, will be automatically enabled on Hopper architecture.
    """
    if (
        _has_flashinfer
        and input.dtype in _FLASHINFER_NORM_SUPPORTED_DTYPES
        and not torch.compiler.is_dynamo_compiling()
    ):
        _flashinfer_norm.gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm(
            input, residual, weight, eps, enable_pdl
        )
    else:
        _gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm_internal(input, residual, weight, eps, enable_pdl)


def _check_shape(input: torch.Tensor, output: torch.Tensor) -> None:
    assert input.ndim == output.ndim, f"{input.ndim} != {output.ndim}"
    assert (
        input.shape[:-1] == output.shape[:-1]
    ), f"{input.shape[:-1]} != {output.shape[:-1]}"
    assert (
        input.shape[-1] == 2 * output.shape[-1]
    ), f"{input.shape[-1]} != {2 * output.shape[-1]}"


def silu_and_mul(input: torch.Tensor, out: torch.Tensor = None) -> torch.Tensor:
    if input.shape[-1] * input.dtype.itemsize % 16 != 0:
        raise ValueError("The pointers must be multiple of 16 bytes.")
    if out is not None:
        _check_shape(input, out)
    else:
        out = torch.empty(
            input.shape[:-1] + (input.shape[-1] // 2,),
            device=input.device,
            dtype=input.dtype,
        )
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.silu_and_mul.default(out, input)
    return out


def gelu_tanh_and_mul(input: torch.Tensor, out: torch.Tensor = None) -> torch.Tensor:
    if input.shape[-1] * input.dtype.itemsize % 16 != 0:
        raise ValueError("The pointers must be multiple of 16 bytes.")
    if out is not None:
        _check_shape(input, out)
    else:
        out = torch.empty(
            input.shape[:-1] + (input.shape[-1] // 2,),
            device=input.device,
            dtype=input.dtype,
        )
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.gelu_tanh_and_mul.default(out, input)
    return out


def gelu_and_mul(input: torch.Tensor, out: torch.Tensor = None) -> torch.Tensor:
    if input.shape[-1] * input.dtype.itemsize % 16 != 0:
        raise ValueError("The pointers must be multiple of 16 bytes.")
    if out is not None:
        _check_shape(input, out)
    else:
        out = torch.empty(
            input.shape[:-1] + (input.shape[-1] // 2,),
            device=input.device,
            dtype=input.dtype,
        )
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.gelu_and_mul.default(out, input)
    return out


if torch.version.hip is not None:

    def gelu_quick(input: torch.Tensor, out: torch.Tensor = None) -> torch.Tensor:
        """
        Quick-GELU:  y = x * sigmoid(1.702 * x)

        The CUDA/HIP kernel uses 128-bit (16-byte) vector loads & stores,
        so the last-dimension byte length must be a multiple of 16 bytes.
        """
        if input.shape[-1] * input.dtype.itemsize % 16 != 0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"The last dimension ({input.shape[-1]}) x itemsize "
                f"({input.dtype.itemsize}) must be a multiple of 16 bytes."
            )

        if out is not None:
            assert input.shape == out.shape, f"{input.shape} != {out.shape}"
        else:
            out = torch.empty_like(input)

        torch.ops.sgl_kernel.gelu_quick(out, input)
        return out


def dsv4_fused_q_norm_rope(
    q_input: torch.Tensor,
    freqs_cis: torch.Tensor,
    positions: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float = 1e-6,
    q_output: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """DeepSeek-V4 fused Q RMSNorm (no weight) + RoPE.

    Parameters
    ----------
    q_input  : (B, num_q_heads, head_dim) bfloat16
    freqs_cis: (max_pos, rope_dim) float32, re/im interleaved
    positions: (B,) int32
    eps      : RMSNorm epsilon
    q_output : optional pre-allocated output tensor
    """
    if q_output is None:
        q_output = torch.empty_like(q_input)
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.dsv4_fused_q_norm_rope.default(
        q_input, q_output, freqs_cis, positions, eps
    )
    return q_output


def dsv4_fused_k_norm_rope_flashmla(
    kv: torch.Tensor,
    kv_weight: torch.Tensor,
    freqs_cis: torch.Tensor,
    positions: torch.Tensor,
    out_loc: torch.Tensor,
    kvcache: torch.Tensor,
    eps: float = 1e-6,
    page_size: int = 1,
) -> None:
    """DeepSeek-V4 fused K RMSNorm + RoPE + FlashMLA FP8 store.

    Parameters
    ----------
    kv       : (B, 512) bfloat16
    kv_weight: (512,) bfloat16
    freqs_cis: (max_pos, 64) float32
    positions: (B,) int32
    out_loc  : (B,) int32  cache slot ids
    kvcache  : (npages, page_bytes) uint8
    eps      : RMSNorm epsilon
    page_size: page size (power of 2)
    """
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.dsv4_fused_k_norm_rope_flashmla.default(
        kv, kv_weight, freqs_cis, positions, out_loc, kvcache, eps, page_size
    )


def dsv4_fused_q_indexer_rope_hadamard_quant(
    q_input: torch.Tensor,
    q_fp8: torch.Tensor,
    weight: torch.Tensor,
    weights_out: torch.Tensor,
    weight_scale: float,
    freqs_cis: torch.Tensor,
    positions: torch.Tensor,
) -> None:
    """DeepSeek-V4 fused Q indexer: RoPE + Hadamard + FP8 quant.

    Parameters
    ----------
    q_input    : (B, num_heads, 128) bfloat16
    q_fp8      : (B, num_heads, 128) fp8_e4m3 output
    weight     : (B, num_heads) bfloat16
    weights_out: (B, num_heads, 1) float32 output
    weight_scale: scalar
    freqs_cis  : (max_pos, 64) float32
    positions  : (B,) int32
    """
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.dsv4_fused_q_indexer_rope_hadamard_quant.default(
        q_input, q_fp8, weight, weights_out, weight_scale, freqs_cis, positions
    )


def rotary_embedding(
    positions: torch.Tensor,
    query: torch.Tensor,
    key: torch.Tensor,
    head_size: int,
    cos_sin_cache: torch.Tensor,
    is_neox: bool = True,
):
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.rotary_embedding.default(
        positions, query, key, head_size, cos_sin_cache, is_neox
    )


def copy_to_gpu_no_ce(input: torch.Tensor, output: torch.Tensor):
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.copy_to_gpu_no_ce(input, output)


def concat_mla_k(
    k: torch.Tensor,
    k_nope: torch.Tensor,
    k_rope: torch.Tensor,
):
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.concat_mla_k(k, k_nope, k_rope)


def concat_mla_absorb_q(
    a: torch.Tensor,
    b: torch.Tensor,
):
    *batch_dims, _ = a.shape
    out = torch.empty(
        (*batch_dims, a.shape[-1] + b.shape[-1]), device=a.device, dtype=a.dtype
    )
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.concat_mla_absorb_q(a, b, out)
    return out

content.thinking:
Now I have an excellent understanding. The architecture is clear:

**Extensions (compiled .so modules):**
1. `common_ops` (sm90 + sm100 variants) — main extension via `csrc/common_extension.cc`
2. `flash_ops` — Flash Attention 3 via `csrc/flash_extension.cc`
3. `infllm_ops` — InfLLM-V2 attention via `csrc/infllm_v2/flash_extension.cc`
4. `flashmla_ops` — FlashMLA via `csrc/flashmla_extension.cc`
5. `spatial_ops` — green context streams via `csrc/spatial_extension.cc`
6. ROCm/MUSA/CPU variants via `common_extension_rocm.cc`, `common_extension_musa.cc`, `cpu/torch_extension_cpu.cpp`

**The kernel pipeline (end-to-end):**
1. CUDA kernel implementation in `csrc/<module>/<name>.cu`
2. C++ function signature declared in `include/sgl_kernel_ops.h`
3. Torch op schema + impl registered in `csrc/common_extension.cc` via `TORCH_LIBRARY_FRAGMENT(sgl_kernel, m)` with `m.def(...)` + `m.impl(...)`
4. Python wrapper in `python/sgl_kernel/<module>.py` calling `torch.ops.sgl_kernel.<name>`
5. Re-export from `python/sgl_kernel/__init__.py`
6. Build via CMakeLists.txt (`SOURCES` list), `make build`
7. Test in `tests/test_<name>.py`, benchmark in `benchmark/bench_<name>.py`

**Key mechanisms:**
- Architecture-specific loading: `load_utils.py` picks sm90 vs sm100 based on compute capability
- `torch.compile` schema support via `m.def` with schema strings
- PDL (Programmatic Dependent Launch) on Hopper
- `make_pytorch_shim` for int→int64 conversion
- FlashInfer fallback in Python wrappers
- Multi-backend: NVIDIA CUDA, AMD ROCm, MUSA, CPU (x86_64/aarch64), Metal (macOS)

Now let me look at a few more representative kernel internals to design good lectures. Let me explore:
- A real CUDA kernel to understand the launch pattern (rmsnorm or fused_add_rms_norm)
- The allreduce (custom communication) — important advanced topic
- The MoE kernels (topk_softmax, moe_align_block_size) — important for LLM
- The speculative sampling
- The cutlass MLA / attention
- The GEMM (fp8/int8)
- The test + benchmark patterns
- The Python wrappers for a few key modules (moe.py, top_k.py, speculative.py, attention.py)

Let me do a batch read of the most important ones. Given budget, I'll be strategic. Let me read several Python wrappers and a couple CUDA kernels, plus a test and benchmark.

Let me focus on what makes good lectures. I'll read:
- python/sgl_kernel/moe.py
- python/sgl_kernel/top_k.py  
- python/sgl_kernel/attention.py
- python/sgl_kernel/gemm.py (partial)
- tests/conftest.py + a test example
- benchmark/bench_rmsnorm.py
- python/sgl_kernel/debug_utils.py
- python/sgl_kernel/utils.py
- csrc/elementwise/fused_add_rms_norm_kernel.cu (a real kernel, partial)

Let me batch these.

from typing import Optional

import torch


def moe_align_block_size(
    topk_ids,
    num_experts,
    block_size,
    sorted_token_ids,
    experts_ids,
    num_tokens_post_pad,
    cumsum_buffer,
    pad_sorted_token_ids=False,
    ignore_invalid_expert=False,
):
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.moe_align_block_size.default(
        topk_ids,
        num_experts,
        block_size,
        sorted_token_ids,
        experts_ids,
        num_tokens_post_pad,
        cumsum_buffer,
        pad_sorted_token_ids,
        ignore_invalid_expert,
    )


def topk_softmax(
    topk_weights: torch.Tensor,
    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,
    gating_output: torch.Tensor,
    renormalize: bool = False,
    moe_softcapping: float = 0.0,
    correction_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> None:
    """
    Compute top-k softmax for MoE routing.

    Args:
        topk_weights: Output tensor for top-k weights [num_tokens, topk]
        topk_ids: Output tensor for top-k expert indices [num_tokens, topk]
        gating_output: Gating logits [num_tokens, num_experts]
        renormalize: Whether to renormalize the top-k weights
        moe_softcapping: Tanh softcapping value (0.0 to disable)
        correction_bias: Per-expert bias correction [num_experts], must be float32 if provided
    """
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.topk_softmax.default(
        topk_weights,
        topk_ids,
        gating_output,
        renormalize,
        moe_softcapping,
        correction_bias,
    )


def topk_sigmoid(
    topk_weights: torch.Tensor,
    topk_ids: torch.Tensor,
    gating_output: torch.Tensor,
    renormalize: bool = False,
    correction_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> None:
    """
    Compute top-k sigmoid for MoE routing.

    Args:
        topk_weights: Output tensor for top-k weights [num_tokens, topk]
        topk_ids: Output tensor for top-k expert indices [num_tokens, topk]
        gating_output: Gating logits [num_tokens, num_experts]
        renormalize: Whether to renormalize the top-k weights
        correction_bias: Per-expert bias correction [num_experts], must be float32 if provided
    """
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.topk_sigmoid.default(
        topk_weights,
        topk_ids,
        gating_output,
        renormalize,
        correction_bias,
    )


def moe_sum_reduce(
    input_tensor,
    output_tensor,
    routed_scaling_factor=0,
):
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.moe_sum_reduce.default(
        input_tensor,
        output_tensor,
        routed_scaling_factor,
    )


def moe_sum(
    input_tensor: torch.Tensor,
    output_tensor: torch.Tensor,
):
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.moe_sum.default(
        input_tensor,
        output_tensor,
    )


# moe_fused_gate / kimi_k2_moe_fused_gate (AOT gate kernels) retired — the gate/topk
# path is consolidated onto the unified Triton router in
# python/sglang/jit_kernel/moe_fused_gate.py (sglang issue #26771).


def fp8_blockwise_scaled_grouped_mm(
    output,
    a_ptrs,
    b_ptrs,
    out_ptrs,
    a_scales_ptrs,
    b_scales_ptrs,
    a,
    b,
    scales_a,
    scales_b,
    stride_a,
    stride_b,
    stride_c,
    layout_sfa,
    layout_sfb,
    problem_sizes,
    expert_offsets,
    workspace,
):
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.fp8_blockwise_scaled_grouped_mm.default(
        output,
        a_ptrs,
        b_ptrs,
        out_ptrs,
        a_scales_ptrs,
        b_scales_ptrs,
        a,
        b,
        scales_a,
        scales_b,
        stride_a,
        stride_b,
        stride_c,
        layout_sfa,
        layout_sfb,
        problem_sizes,
        expert_offsets,
        workspace,
    )


def prepare_moe_input(
    topk_ids,
    expert_offsets,
    problem_sizes1,
    problem_sizes2,
    input_permutation,
    output_permutation,
    num_experts,
    n,
    k,
    blockscale_offsets: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
):
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.prepare_moe_input.default(
        topk_ids,
        expert_offsets,
        blockscale_offsets,
        problem_sizes1,
        problem_sizes2,
        input_permutation,
        output_permutation,
        num_experts,
        n,
        k,
    )


def apply_shuffle_mul_sum(
    input,
    output,
    permutation,
    factors,
):
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.apply_shuffle_mul_sum.default(
        input, output, permutation, factors
    )


def fused_qk_norm_rope(
    qkv: torch.Tensor,
    num_heads_q: int,
    num_heads_k: int,
    num_heads_v: int,
    head_dim: int,
    eps: float,
    q_weight: torch.Tensor,
    k_weight: torch.Tensor,
    base: float,
    is_neox: bool,
    position_ids: torch.Tensor,
    factor: float,
    low: float,
    high: float,
    attention_factor: float,
    rotary_dim: Optional[int] = None,
) -> None:
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.fused_qk_norm_rope(
        qkv,
        num_heads_q,
        num_heads_k,
        num_heads_v,
        head_dim,
        eps,
        q_weight,
        k_weight,
        base,
        is_neox,
        position_ids,
        factor,
        low,
        high,
        attention_factor,
        rotary_dim if rotary_dim is not None else head_dim,
    )

15	
16	import functools
17	from typing import Dict, Tuple
18	
19	import torch
20	
21	_cache_buf: Dict[Tuple[str, torch.device], torch.Tensor] = {}
22	
23	
24	def _get_cache_buf(name: str, bytes: int, device: torch.device) -> torch.Tensor:
25	    key = (name, device)
26	    buf = _cache_buf.get(key)
27	    if buf is None:
28	        buf = torch.empty(bytes, dtype=torch.uint8, device=device)
29	        _cache_buf[key] = buf
30	    return buf
31	
32	
33	def _to_tensor_scalar_tuple(x):
34	    if isinstance(x, torch.Tensor):
35	        return (x, 0)
36	    else:
37	        return (None, x)
38	
39	
40	def cache_once(fn):
41	    """
42	    NOTE: `functools.lru_cache` is not compatible with `torch.compile`
43	    So we manually implement a simple cache_once decorator to replace it.
44	    """
45	    result_map = {}
46	
47	    @functools.wraps(fn)
48	    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
49	        key = (args, tuple(sorted(kwargs.items())))
50	        if key not in result_map:
51	            result_map[key] = fn(*args, **kwargs)
52	        return result_map[key]
53	
54	    return wrapper
55	
56	
57	@cache_once
58	def is_arch_support_pdl() -> bool:
59	    if getattr(torch.version, "hip", None) or getattr(torch.version, "musa", None):
60	        return False
61	    try:
62	        device = torch.cuda.current_device()
63	        major, _ = torch.cuda.get_device_capability(device)
64	    except Exception:
65	        return False
66	    return major >= 9
67

1	import os
2	from typing import Any, Callable, TypeVar, cast, overload
3	
4	F = TypeVar("F", bound=Callable[..., Any])
5	
6	
7	def _wrap_debug_kernel(func: F, op_name: str | None = None) -> F:
8	    try:
9	        if int(os.environ.get("SGLANG_KERNEL_API_LOGLEVEL", "0")) == 0:
10	            return func
11	    except Exception:
12	        return func
13	
14	    try:
15	        from sglang.kernel_api_logging import debug_kernel_api
16	    except Exception:
17	        return func
18	
19	    if getattr(func, "_debug_kernel_wrapped", False):
20	        return func
21	
22	    wrapped = debug_kernel_api(func, op_name=op_name)
23	    setattr(wrapped, "_debug_kernel_wrapped", True)
24	    return cast(F, wrapped)
25	
26	
27	@overload
28	def maybe_wrap_debug_kernel(func: F) -> F: ...
29	
30	
31	@overload
32	def maybe_wrap_debug_kernel(func: F, op_name: str) -> F: ...
33	
34	
35	@overload
36	def maybe_wrap_debug_kernel(*, op_name: str | None = None) -> Callable[[F], F]: ...
37	
38	
39	def maybe_wrap_debug_kernel(
40	    func: F | None = None, op_name: str | None = None
41	) -> F | Callable[[F], F]:
42	    if func is None:
43	        return lambda wrapped_func: _wrap_debug_kernel(wrapped_func, op_name)
44	
45	    return _wrap_debug_kernel(func, op_name)

1	# Benchmarks SGLang RMSNorm kernels versus vLLM and FlashInfer across
2	# (batch_size, seq_len, hidden_size) and prints speed-up.
3	import argparse
4	import itertools
5	import os
6	import re
7	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple, Union
8	
9	import sgl_kernel
10	import torch
11	import torch.nn as nn
12	import triton
13	import triton.testing
14	from sgl_kernel.utils import is_arch_support_pdl
15	
16	from sglang.utils import is_in_ci
17	
18	# Optional imports
19	try:
20	    from flashinfer.norm import fused_add_rmsnorm, rmsnorm
21	
22	    FLASHINFER_AVAILABLE = True
23	except ImportError:
24	    fused_add_rmsnorm = None
25	    rmsnorm = None
26	    FLASHINFER_AVAILABLE = False
27	
28	try:
29	    from vllm import _custom_ops as vllm_ops
30	
31	    VLLM_AVAILABLE = True
32	except ImportError:
33	    vllm_ops = None
34	    VLLM_AVAILABLE = False
35	
36	IS_CI = is_in_ci()
37	
38	
39	def str2int_list(arg: str) -> List[int]:
40	    if arg in ("", None):
41	        return []
42	    if re.fullmatch(r"\d+(,\d+)*", arg.strip()) is None:
43	        raise argparse.ArgumentTypeError(f"Bad int list: {arg}")
44	    return [int(x) for x in arg.split(",")]
45	
46	
47	class HuggingFaceRMSNorm(nn.Module):
48	    def __init__(self, hidden_size: int, eps: float = 1e-6) -> None:
49	        super().__init__()
50	        self.weight = nn.Parameter(torch.ones(hidden_size))
51	        self.variance_epsilon = eps
52	
53	    def forward(
54	        self,
55	        x: torch.Tensor,
56	        residual: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
57	    ) -> Union[torch.Tensor, Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]]:
58	        orig_dtype = x.dtype
59	        x = x.to(torch.float32)
60	        if residual is not None:
61	            x = x + residual.to(torch.float32)
62	            residual = x.to(orig_dtype)
63	
64	        variance = x.pow(2).mean(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
65	        x = x * torch.rsqrt(variance + self.variance_epsilon)
66	        x = x.to(orig_dtype) * self.weight
67	        if residual is None:
68	            return x
69	        else:
70	            return x, residual
71	
72	
73	def rmsnorm_naive(
74	    x: torch.Tensor,
75	    weight: torch.Tensor,
76	    residual: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
77	    eps: float = 1e-6,
78	):
79	    naive_norm = HuggingFaceRMSNorm(x.shape[-1], eps=eps)
80	    naive_norm.weight = nn.Parameter(weight)
81	    naive_norm = naive_norm.to(x.device)
82	
83	    orig_shape = x.shape
84	    x = x.view(-1, x.shape[-1])
85	    if residual is not None:
86	        residual = residual.view(-1, residual.shape[-1])
87	
88	    output = naive_norm(x, residual)
89	
90	    if isinstance(output, tuple):
91	        output = (output[0].view(orig_shape), output[1].view(orig_shape))
92	    else:
93	        output = output.view(orig_shape)
94	    return output
95	
96	
97	def rmsnorm_flashinfer(
98	    x: torch.Tensor,
99	    weight: torch.Tensor,
100	    residual: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
101	    eps: float = 1e-6,
102	):
103	    if not FLASHINFER_AVAILABLE:
104	        # Fallback to naive implementation if FlashInfer is not available
105	        return rmsnorm_naive(x, weight, residual, eps)
106	
107	    orig_shape = x.shape
108	    x = x.view(-1, x.shape[-1])
109	    if residual is not None:
110	        residual = residual.view(-1, residual.shape[-1])
111	
112	    if residual is not None:
113	        fused_add_rmsnorm(x, residual, weight, eps)
114	        output = (x, residual)
115	    else:
116	        output = rmsnorm(x, weight, eps)
117	
118	    if isinstance(output, tuple):
119	        output = (output[0].view(orig_shape), output[1].view(orig_shape))
120	    else:
121	        output = output.view(orig_shape)
122	    return output
123	
124	
125	def rmsnorm_vllm(
126	    x: torch.Tensor,
127	    weight: torch.Tensor,
128	    residual: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
129	    eps: float = 1e-6,
130	):
131	    if not VLLM_AVAILABLE:
132	        # Fallback to naive implementation if vLLM is not available
133	        return rmsnorm_naive(x, weight, residual, eps)
134	
135	    orig_shape = x.shape
136	    x = x.view(-1, x.shape[-1])
137	    if residual is not None:
138	        residual = residual.view(-1, residual.shape[-1])
139	
140	    if residual is not None:
141	        vllm_ops.fused_add_rms_norm(x, residual, weight, eps)
142	        output = (x, residual)
143	    else:
144	        out = torch.empty_like(x)
145	        vllm_ops.rms_norm(out, x, weight, eps)
146	        output = out
147	
148	    if isinstance(output, tuple):
149	        output = (output[0].view(orig_shape), output[1].view(orig_shape))
150	    else:
151	        output = output.view(orig_shape)
152	    return output
153	
154	
155	def rmsnorm_sglang(
156	    x: torch.Tensor,
157	    weight: torch.Tensor,
158	    residual: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
159	    eps: float = 1e-6,
160	    enable_pdl: Optional[bool] = None,
161	):
162	    orig_shape = x.shape
163	    x = x.view(-1, x.shape[-1])
164	    if residual is not None:
165	        residual = residual.view(-1, residual.shape[-1])
166	
167	    if enable_pdl is None:
168	        enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
169	
170	    if residual is not None:
171	        sgl_kernel.fused_add_rmsnorm(x, residual, weight, eps, enable_pdl=enable_pdl)
172	        output = (x, residual)
173	    else:
174	        out = torch.empty_like(x)
175	        sgl_kernel.rmsnorm(x, weight, eps, out=out, enable_pdl=enable_pdl)
176	        output = out
177	
178	    if isinstance(output, tuple):
179	        output = (output[0].view(orig_shape), output[1].view(orig_shape))
180	    else:
181	        output = output.view(orig_shape)
182	    return output
183	
184	
185	def calculate_diff(batch_size, seq_len, hidden_size, use_residual=True):
186	    dtype = torch.bfloat16
187	    x = torch.randn(batch_size, seq_len, hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")
188	    weight = torch.ones(hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")
189	    residual = torch.randn_like(x) if use_residual else None
190	
191	    output_naive = rmsnorm_naive(
192	        x.clone(), weight, residual.clone() if residual is not None else None
193	    )
194	    output_flashinfer = rmsnorm_flashinfer(
195	        x.clone(), weight, residual.clone() if residual is not None else None
196	    )
197	    output_vllm = rmsnorm_vllm(
198	        x.clone(), weight, residual.clone() if residual is not None else None
199	    )
200	    output_sglang = rmsnorm_sglang(
201	        x.clone(), weight, residual.clone() if residual is not None else None
202	    )
203	
204	    if use_residual:
205	        output_naive = output_naive[0]
206	        output_flashinfer = output_flashinfer[0]
207	        output_vllm = output_vllm[0]
208	        output_sglang = output_sglang[0]
209	
210	    print(f"Naive output={output_naive}")
211	    if FLASHINFER_AVAILABLE:
212	        print(f"FlashInfer output={output_flashinfer}")
213	    else:
214	        print("FlashInfer not available, skipped")
215	    if VLLM_AVAILABLE:
216	        print(f"VLLM output={output_vllm}")
217	    else:
218	        print("vLLM not available, skipped")
219	    print(f"SGLang output={output_sglang}")
220	
221	    # Only compare available implementations
222	    all_match = torch.allclose(output_naive, output_sglang, atol=1e-2, rtol=1e-2)
223	    if FLASHINFER_AVAILABLE:
224	        all_match = all_match and torch.allclose(
225	            output_naive, output_flashinfer, atol=1e-2, rtol=1e-2
226	        )
227	    if VLLM_AVAILABLE:
228	        all_match = all_match and torch.allclose(
229	            output_naive, output_vllm, atol=1e-2, rtol=1e-2
230	        )
231	
232	    if all_match:
233	        print("✅ All available implementations match")
234	    else:
235	        print("❌ Implementations differ")
236	
237	
238	# CI environment uses simplified parameters
239	if IS_CI:
240	    default_batch_sizes = [1]  # Single batch size for CI
241	    default_seq_lens = [64]  # Single sequence length for CI
242	    default_hidden_sizes = [4096]  # Single hidden size for CI
243	else:
244	    default_batch_sizes = [2**i for i in range(0, 7, 2)]  # 1, 4, 16, 64
245	    default_seq_lens = [2**i for i in range(6, 11, 1)]  # 64, 128, 256, 512, 1024
246	    default_hidden_sizes = [32 * 128, 48 * 128]  # 4096, 6144
247	
248	
249	def make_configs(bsizes: List[int], slens: List[int], hsizes: List[int]) -> List[Tuple]:
250	    return list(itertools.product(bsizes, slens, hsizes))
251	
252	
253	# Filter providers based on availability
254	available_providers = ["huggingface", "sglang"]
255	available_names = ["HuggingFace", "SGL Kernel"]
256	available_styles = [("blue", "-"), ("orange", "-")]
257	
258	if FLASHINFER_AVAILABLE:
259	    available_providers.insert(-1, "flashinfer")
260	    available_names.insert(-1, "FlashInfer")
261	    available_styles.insert(-1, ("green", "-"))
262	
263	if VLLM_AVAILABLE:
264	    available_providers.insert(-1, "vllm")
265	    available_names.insert(-1, "vLLM")
266	    available_styles.insert(-1, ("red", "-"))
267	
268	
269	@triton.testing.perf_report(
270	    triton.testing.Benchmark(
271	        x_names=["batch_size", "seq_len", "hidden_size"],
272	        x_vals=[],
273	        line_arg="provider",
274	        line_vals=available_providers,
275	        line_names=available_names,
276	        styles=available_styles,
277	        ylabel="µs (median)  or  × (speed-up)",
278	        plot_name="rmsnorm-performance",
279	        args={},
280	    )
281	)
282	def benchmark(batch_size, seq_len, hidden_size, provider, use_residual):
283	    device = torch.device("cuda")
284	    dtype = torch.bfloat16
285	
286	    x = torch.randn(batch_size, seq_len, hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device=device)
287	    weight = torch.ones(hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device=device)
288	    residual = torch.randn_like(x) if use_residual else None
289	
290	    # timing helper
291	    def timed(fn):
292	        for _ in range(5):
293	            fn()
294	        torch.cuda.synchronize()
295	        ms, qmin, qmax = triton.testing.do_bench_cudagraph(
296	            fn, quantiles=[0.5, 0.2, 0.8]
297	        )
298	        return 1000 * ms, 1000 * qmax, 1000 * qmin
299	
300	    if provider == "huggingface":
301	        return timed(
302	            lambda: rmsnorm_naive(
303	                x.clone(),
304	                weight,
305	                residual.clone() if residual is not None else None,
306	            )
307	        )
308	    elif provider == "flashinfer":
309	        if not FLASHINFER_AVAILABLE:
310	            return (0, 0, 0)
311	        return timed(
312	            lambda: rmsnorm_flashinfer(
313	                x.clone(),
314	                weight,
315	                residual.clone() if residual is not None else None,
316	            )
317	        )
318	    elif provider == "vllm":
319	        if not VLLM_AVAILABLE:
320	            return (0, 0, 0)
321	        return timed(
322	            lambda: rmsnorm_vllm(
323	                x.clone(),
324	                weight,
325	                residual.clone() if residual is not None else None,
326	            )
327	        )
328	    elif provider == "sglang":
329	        return timed(
330	            lambda: rmsnorm_sglang(
331	                x.clone(),
332	                weight,
333	                residual.clone() if residual is not None else None,
334	            )
335	        )
336	
337	    # provider == "speedup"
338	    if VLLM_AVAILABLE:
339	        t_ref, _, _ = timed(
340	            lambda: rmsnorm_vllm(
341	                x.clone(),
342	                weight,
343	                residual.clone() if residual is not None else None,
344	            )
345	        )
346	    else:
347	        t_ref, _, _ = timed(
348	            lambda: rmsnorm_naive(
349	                x.clone(),
350	                weight,
351	                residual.clone() if residual is not None else None,
352	            )
353	        )
354	    t_sgl, _, _ = timed(
355	        lambda: rmsnorm_sglang(
356	            x.clone(),
357	            weight,
358	            residual.clone() if residual is not None else None,
359	        )
360	    )
361	    spd = t_ref / t_sgl if t_ref > 0 else 1.0
362	    return (spd, spd, spd)
363	
364	
365	if __name__ == "__main__":
366	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser("RMSNorm kernel benchmark")
367	    p.add_argument("--batch_sizes", type=str2int_list, default=default_batch_sizes)
368	    p.add_argument("--seq_lens", type=str2int_list, default=default_seq_lens)
369	    p.add_argument("--hidden_sizes", type=str2int_list, default=default_hidden_sizes)
370	    p.add_argument(
371	        "--use_residual", action="store_true", help="Whether to use residual connection"
372	    )
373	    p.add_argument("--verify_only", action="store_true")
374	    args = p.parse_args()
375	
376	    # coerce lists
377	    if isinstance(args.batch_sizes, str):
378	        args.batch_sizes = str2int_list(args.batch_sizes)
379	    if isinstance(args.seq_lens, str):
380	        args.seq_lens = str2int_list(args.seq_lens)
381	    if isinstance(args.hidden_sizes, str):
382	        args.hidden_sizes = str2int_list(args.hidden_sizes)
383	
384	    # patch perf_report grid
385	    benchmark_grid = make_configs(args.batch_sizes, args.seq_lens, args.hidden_sizes)
386	    if hasattr(benchmark, "benchmarks"):
387	        benchmark.benchmarks.x_vals = benchmark_grid
388	    else:
389	        benchmark.benchmark.x_vals = benchmark_grid
390

392	        ok = calculate_diff(4, 128, args.hidden_sizes[0], args.use_residual)
393	        print("✅ sanity pass" if ok else "❌ mismatch")

# Adapted from https://github.com/flashinfer-ai/flashinfer/blob/4e8eb1879f9c3ba6d75511e5893183bf8f289a62/tests/test_norm.py

import sys

import pytest
import sgl_kernel
import torch
from sgl_kernel.utils import is_arch_support_pdl


def llama_rms_norm(x, w, eps=1e-6):
    orig_dtype = x.dtype
    x = x.float()
    variance = x.pow(2).mean(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    x = x * torch.rsqrt(variance + eps)
    x = x * w.float()
    x = x.to(orig_dtype)
    return x


def gemma_rms_norm(x, w, eps=1e-6):
    orig_dtype = x.dtype
    x = x.float()
    variance = x.pow(2).mean(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    x = x * torch.rsqrt(variance + eps)
    x = x * (1.0 + w.float())
    x = x.to(orig_dtype)
    return x


def gemma_fused_add_rms_norm(x, residual, w, eps=1e-6):
    orig_dtype = x.dtype
    x = x + residual
    residual = x
    x = x.float()
    variance = x.pow(2).mean(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    x = x * torch.rsqrt(variance + eps)
    x = x * (1.0 + w.float())
    x = x.to(orig_dtype)
    return x, residual


def fused_add_rms_norm(x, residual, weight, eps):
    orig_dtype = x.dtype
    x = x.to(torch.float32)
    x = x + residual.to(torch.float32)
    residual = x.to(orig_dtype)

    variance = x.pow(2).mean(dim=-1, keepdim=True)
    x = x * torch.rsqrt(variance + eps)
    x = (x * weight.float()).to(orig_dtype)
    return x, residual


def assert_close_norm(actual, expected, dtype):
    if dtype is torch.bfloat16:
        torch.testing.assert_close(actual, expected, rtol=1e-2, atol=2e-2)
    else:
        torch.testing.assert_close(actual, expected, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)


@pytest.mark.parametrize("batch_size", [1, 19, 99, 989])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("hidden_size", [111, 500, 1024, 3072, 3584, 4096, 8192, 16384])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("dtype", [torch.float16])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("specify_out", [True, False])
def test_norm(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype, specify_out):
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, hidden_size).to(0).to(dtype)
    w = torch.randn(hidden_size).to(0).to(dtype)

    y_ref = llama_rms_norm(x, w)
    enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    if specify_out:
        y = torch.empty_like(x)
        sgl_kernel.rmsnorm(x, w, out=y, enable_pdl=enable_pdl)
    else:
        y = sgl_kernel.rmsnorm(x, w, enable_pdl=enable_pdl)

    torch.testing.assert_close(y_ref, y, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)


@pytest.mark.parametrize("batch_size", [1, 19, 99, 989])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("hidden_size", [111, 500, 1024, 3072, 3584, 4096, 8192, 16384])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("dtype", [torch.float16, torch.float32])
def test_fused_add_rmsnorm(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype):
    eps = 1e-6

    x = torch.randn(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")
    residual = torch.randn_like(x)
    weight = torch.randn(hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")

    x_native, residual_native = fused_add_rms_norm(
        x.clone(), residual.clone(), weight, eps
    )

    x_fused = x.clone()
    residual_fused = residual.clone()
    enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    sgl_kernel.fused_add_rmsnorm(
        x_fused, residual_fused, weight, eps, enable_pdl=enable_pdl
    )

    torch.testing.assert_close(x_fused, x_native, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)
    torch.testing.assert_close(residual_fused, residual_native, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)


PRODUCTION_LIKE_NORM_CASES = [
    (38, 4096, torch.bfloat16),
    (1240, 1536, torch.bfloat16),
    (7807, 128, torch.bfloat16),
]


@pytest.mark.parametrize("batch_size,hidden_size,dtype", PRODUCTION_LIKE_NORM_CASES)
def test_norm_production_like_shapes(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype):
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")
    w = torch.randn(hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")

    y_ref = llama_rms_norm(x, w)
    enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    y = sgl_kernel.rmsnorm(x, w, enable_pdl=enable_pdl)

    assert_close_norm(y_ref, y, dtype)


PRODUCTION_LIKE_FUSED_ADD_RMSNORM_CASES = [
    (39, 4096, torch.bfloat16),
    (39, 8192, torch.bfloat16),
    (89, 4096, torch.bfloat16),
]


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "batch_size,hidden_size,dtype", PRODUCTION_LIKE_FUSED_ADD_RMSNORM_CASES
)
def test_fused_add_rmsnorm_production_like_shapes(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype):
    eps = 1e-6

    x = torch.randn(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")
    residual = torch.randn_like(x)
    weight = torch.randn(hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")

    x_native, residual_native = fused_add_rms_norm(
        x.clone(), residual.clone(), weight, eps
    )

    x_fused = x.clone()
    residual_fused = residual.clone()
    enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    sgl_kernel.fused_add_rmsnorm(
        x_fused, residual_fused, weight, eps, enable_pdl=enable_pdl
    )

    assert_close_norm(x_fused, x_native, dtype)
    torch.testing.assert_close(residual_fused, residual_native, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)


@pytest.mark.parametrize("batch_size", [1, 19, 99, 989])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("hidden_size", [111, 500, 1024, 3072, 3584, 4096, 8192, 16384])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("dtype", [torch.float16])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("specify_out", [True, False])
def test_gemma_norm(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype, specify_out):
    x = torch.randn(batch_size, hidden_size).to(0).to(dtype)
    w = torch.randn(hidden_size).to(0).to(dtype)

    y_ref = gemma_rms_norm(x, w)
    enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    if specify_out:
        y = torch.empty_like(x)
        sgl_kernel.gemma_rmsnorm(x, w, out=y, enable_pdl=enable_pdl)
    else:
        y = sgl_kernel.gemma_rmsnorm(x, w, enable_pdl=enable_pdl)

    torch.testing.assert_close(y_ref, y, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)


@pytest.mark.parametrize("batch_size", [1, 19, 99, 989])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("hidden_size", [111, 500, 1024, 3072, 3584, 4096, 8192, 16384])
@pytest.mark.parametrize("dtype", [torch.float16])
def test_gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype):
    eps = 1e-6

    x = torch.randn(batch_size, hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")
    residual = torch.randn_like(x)
    weight = torch.randn(hidden_size, dtype=dtype, device="cuda")

    x_native, residual_native = gemma_fused_add_rms_norm(
        x.clone(), residual.clone(), weight, eps
    )

    x_fused = x.clone()
    residual_fused = residual.clone()
    enable_pdl = is_arch_support_pdl()
    sgl_kernel.gemma_fused_add_rmsnorm(
        x_fused, residual_fused, weight, eps, enable_pdl=enable_pdl
    )

    torch.testing.assert_close(x_fused, x_native, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)
    torch.testing.assert_close(residual_fused, residual_native, rtol=1e-3, atol=1e-3)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    sys.exit(pytest.main([__file__]))

1	from typing import Optional
2	
3	import torch
4	
5	
6	def fast_topk(values, topk, dim):
7	    if topk == 1:
8	        # Use max along the specified dimension to get both value and index
9	        return torch.max(values, dim=dim, keepdim=True)
10	    else:
11	        # Use topk for efficiency with larger k values
12	        # TODO: implement faster cuda kernels for large vocab sizes
13	        return torch.topk(values, topk, dim=dim)
14	
15	
16	def fast_topk_v2(
17	    score: torch.Tensor,
18	    lengths: torch.Tensor,
19	    topk: int,
20	    row_starts: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
21	) -> torch.Tensor:
22	    """
23	    Get the topk indices of the score tensor.
24	    Args:
25	        score: The score tensor of shape (B, L). The score tensor is the logits
26	            between the query and the key whose layout is either ragged or paged.
27	            row_starts is only required when the key is ragged.
28	        lengths: The lengths tensor of shape (B)
29	        topk: The number of topk indices to get
30	        row_starts: The start index of each row in the score tensor of shape (B).
31	            For each row i, topk only applies to section [row_starts[i], row_starts[i] + lengths[i]]
32	            of the score tensor.
33	    Returns:
34	        The topk indices tensor of shape (B, topk)
35	    """
36	    assert (

from typing import Optional

import torch


def fast_topk(values, topk, dim):
    if topk == 1:
        # Use max along the specified dimension to get both value and index
        return torch.max(values, dim=dim, keepdim=True)
    else:
        # Use topk for efficiency with larger k values
        # TODO: implement faster cuda kernels for large vocab sizes
        return torch.topk(values, topk, dim=dim)


def fast_topk_v2(
    score: torch.Tensor,
    lengths: torch.Tensor,
    topk: int,
    row_starts: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Get the topk indices of the score tensor.
    Args:
        score: The score tensor of shape (B, L). The score tensor is the logits
            between the query and the key whose layout is either ragged or paged.
            row_starts is only required when the key is ragged.
        lengths: The lengths tensor of shape (B)
        topk: The number of topk indices to get
        row_starts: The start index of each row in the score tensor of shape (B).
            For each row i, topk only applies to section [row_starts[i], row_starts[i] + lengths[i]]
            of the score tensor.
    Returns:
        The topk indices tensor of shape (B, topk)
    """
    assert (
        topk == 2048
    ), "fast_topk_v2 is only optimized for deepseek v3.2 model, where topk=2048"
    assert score.dim() == 2
    topk_indices = score.new_empty((score.size(0), topk), dtype=torch.int32)
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.fast_topk(score, topk_indices, lengths, row_starts)
    return topk_indices


def fast_topk_transform_fused(
    score: torch.Tensor,
    lengths: torch.Tensor,
    page_table_size_1: torch.Tensor,  # NOTE: page size should be 1
    cu_seqlens_q: torch.Tensor,
    topk: int,
    row_starts: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Get the topk indices of the score tensor and then transform the topk indices
    to indices to the page table (page_size = 1)
    Args:
        score: The score tensor of shape (B, L). The score tensor is the logits
            between the query and the key whose layout is either ragged or paged.
            row_starts is only required when the key is ragged.
        lengths: The lengths tensor of shape (B)
        page_table_size_1: The page table tensor of shape (Batch, topk)
        cu_seqlens_q: The cumulative sequence lengths tensor of shape (Batch + 1)
        topk: The number of topk indices to get
        row_starts: The start index of each row in the score tensor of shape (B).
            For each row i, topk only applies to section [row_starts[i], row_starts[i] + lengths[i]]
            of the score tensor. It's only used for cases where the key is
            ragged, i.e. during extend and draft extend.
    Returns:
        The topk indices tensor of shape (B, topk)
    """
    assert (
        topk == 2048
    ), "fast_topk_transform_fused is only optimized for deepseek v3.2 model, where topk=2048"
    assert score.dim() == 2
    src_page_table = page_table_size_1
    dst_page_table = score.new_empty((score.shape[0], topk), dtype=torch.int32)
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.fast_topk_transform_fused(
        score, lengths, dst_page_table, src_page_table, cu_seqlens_q, row_starts
    )
    return dst_page_table


def deepseek_v4_topk_transform_512(
    scores: torch.Tensor,
    seq_lens: torch.Tensor,
    page_table: torch.Tensor,
    page_indices: torch.Tensor,
    page_size: int,
    raw_indices: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> None:
    """
    Performs the DeepSeek-V4 indexer top-k selection and writes the paged
    physical slot indices into ``page_indices``. Supports topk up to 1024.
    Optionally also writes the row-relative raw token positions into
    ``raw_indices`` for hisparse capture.

    Args:
        scores: float32 ``[B, max_seq_len]`` indexer logits, contiguous on dim 1.
        seq_lens: int32 ``[B]``, true KV length per batch row.
        page_table: int32 ``[B, num_pages]``, logical->physical page table,
            contiguous on dim 1.
        page_indices: int32 ``[B, topk]``, output buffer, contiguous. Filled
            with paged physical slots; -1 for padding entries.
        page_size: power-of-2 page size.
        raw_indices: optional int32 ``[B, topk]``, contiguous. If provided,
            filled with raw token positions within each row.
    """
    if raw_indices is not None:
        assert raw_indices.dim() == 2
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.deepseek_v4_topk_transform_512(
        scores, seq_lens, page_table, page_indices, page_size, raw_indices
    )


def fast_topk_transform_ragged_fused(
    score: torch.Tensor,
    lengths: torch.Tensor,
    topk_indices_offset: torch.Tensor,  # ragged kv
    topk: int,
    row_starts: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    """
    Get the topk indices of the score tensor and then transform the topk indices to
    indices to ragged kv (non-paged). This function is only used for extend,
    not including draft extend.
    Args:
        score: The score tensor of shape (B, L). The score tensor is the logits
            between the query and the key which can be ragged or paged.
            row_starts is only required when the key is ragged.
        lengths: The lengths tensor of shape (B)
        topk_indices_offset: The offset of topk indices in ragged kv of shape (B)
        topk: The number of topk indices to get
        row_starts: The start index of each row in the score tensor of shape (B).
            For each row i, topk only applies to section [row_starts[i], row_starts[i] + lengths[i]]
            of the score tensor. It can be None if only the fast path is triggered,
            in the case of all values in lengths <= topk (not checked in the kernel,
            guaranteed by the caller).
    Returns:
        The topk indices tensor of shape (B, topk)
    """
    assert (
        topk == 2048
    ), "fast_topk_transform_ragged_fused is only optimized for deepseek v3.2 model, where topk=2048"
    assert score.dim() == 2
    topk_indices_ragged = score.new_empty((score.shape[0], topk), dtype=torch.int32)
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.fast_topk_transform_ragged_fused(
        score, lengths, topk_indices_ragged, topk_indices_offset, row_starts
    )
    return topk_indices_ragged

1	from typing import Optional, Tuple
2	
3	import torch
4	
5	
6	def merge_state_v2(
7	    v_a: torch.Tensor,
8	    s_a: torch.Tensor,
9	    v_b: torch.Tensor,
10	    s_b: torch.Tensor,
11	    v_merged: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
12	    s_merged: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
13	) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
14	    s_a = s_a.to(torch.float32)
15	    s_b = s_b.to(torch.float32)
16	    # TODO(DefTruth): Currently, the custom merge_attn_states kernel
17	    # does not support the FP8 data type and non - CUDA devices.
18	    # It may be necessary to fall back to using the Triton kernel.
19	
20	    # Avoid creating new tensors if they are already provided
21	    if v_merged is None:
22	        v_merged = torch.empty_like(v_a)
23	    if s_merged is None:
24	        s_merged = torch.empty_like(s_a)
25	    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.merge_state_v2.default(v_a, s_a, v_b, s_b, v_merged, s_merged)
26	    return v_merged, s_merged
27	
28	
29	def cutlass_mla_decode(
30	    q_nope: torch.Tensor,
31	    q_pe: torch.Tensor,
32	    kv_c_and_k_pe_cache: torch.Tensor,
33	    seq_lens: torch.Tensor,
34	    page_table: torch.Tensor,
35	    workspace: torch.Tensor,
36	    sm_scale: float,
37	    num_kv_splits: int = 1,  # Set to 1 to avoid cuda_graph issue by default.
38	) -> torch.Tensor:
39	    assert q_nope.ndim == 3, f"q_nope must be a 3D tensor, but got {q_nope.ndim}"
40	    assert q_pe.ndim == 3, f"q_pe must be a 3D tensor, but got {q_pe.ndim}"
41	    assert (
42	        kv_c_and_k_pe_cache.ndim == 3
43	    ), f"kv_c_and_k_pe_cache must be a 3D tensor, but got {kv_c_and_k_pe_cache.ndim}"
44	
45	    B_q, H, D_q_nope = q_nope.shape
46	    B_q_2, H_2, D_q_pe = q_pe.shape
47	    assert (B_q == B_q_2) and (H == H_2)
48	
49	    _, PAGE_SIZE, D_ckv = kv_c_and_k_pe_cache.shape
50	
51	    D_latent = 512
52	    D_rope = 64
53	    assert D_q_nope == D_latent
54	    assert D_q_pe == D_rope
55	    assert D_ckv == D_latent + D_rope
56	
57	    MAX_HEADS = 128
58	    assert H <= MAX_HEADS, f"H must be <= {MAX_HEADS}, but got {H}"
59	    if H < MAX_HEADS:
60	        q_nope_padded = q_nope.new_empty((B_q, MAX_HEADS, D_q_nope))
61	        q_nope_padded[:, :H] = q_nope
62	        q_nope = q_nope_padded
63	
64	        q_pe_padded = q_pe.new_empty((B_q, MAX_HEADS, D_q_pe))
65	        q_pe_padded[:, :H] = q_pe
66	        q_pe = q_pe_padded
67	
68	    assert len(page_table.shape) == 2
69	    B_block_table, block_num = page_table.shape
70	    assert B_block_table == B_q
71	    assert block_num > 0, f"block num must be greater than 0, got {block_num}"
72	    assert block_num % (128 / PAGE_SIZE) == 0
73	
74	    # TODO(kaixih@nvidia): support fp8
75	    assert q_nope.dtype in (
76	        torch.float16,
77	        torch.bfloat16,
78	    ), f"q_nope.dtype needs to be fp16 or bf16 but got {q_nope.dtype}."
79	    assert q_nope.dtype == q_pe.dtype == kv_c_and_k_pe_cache.dtype
80	    assert (
81	        seq_lens.dtype == torch.int32
82	    ), f"seq_lens.dtype needs to be int32 but got {seq_lens.dtype}."
83	    assert (
84	        page_table.dtype == torch.int32
85	    ), f"page_table.dtype needs to be int32 but got {page_table.dtype}."
86	
87	    out = q_nope.new_empty((B_q, MAX_HEADS, D_latent))
88	
89	    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.cutlass_mla_decode.default(
90	        out,
91	        q_nope,
92	        q_pe,
93	        kv_c_and_k_pe_cache,
94	        seq_lens,
95	        page_table,
96	        workspace,
97	        sm_scale,
98	        num_kv_splits,
99	    )
100	    return out[:, :H].contiguous()
101	
102	
103	def cutlass_mla_get_workspace_size(
104	    max_seq_len: int,
105	    num_batches: int,
106	    sm_count: int = 0,
107	    num_kv_splits: int = 1,  # Set to 1 to avoid cuda_graph issue by default.
108	) -> int:
109	    assert max_seq_len > 0, f"max_seq_len must be greater than 0, got {max_seq_len}"
110	    assert num_batches > 0, f"num_batches must be greater than 0, got {num_batches}"
111	    return torch.ops.sgl_kernel.cutlass_mla_get_workspace_size.default(
112	        max_seq_len, num_batches, sm_count, num_kv_splits
113	    )

from typing import Optional, Tuple

import torch


def merge_state_v2(
    v_a: torch.Tensor,
    s_a: torch.Tensor,
    v_b: torch.Tensor,
    s_b: torch.Tensor,
    v_merged: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
    s_merged: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> Tuple[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor]:
    s_a = s_a.to(torch.float32)
    s_b = s_b.to(torch.float32)
    # TODO(DefTruth): Currently, the custom merge_attn_states kernel
    # does not support the FP8 data type and non - CUDA devices.
    # It may be necessary to fall back to using the Triton kernel.

    # Avoid creating new tensors if they are already provided
    if v_merged is None:
        v_merged = torch.empty_like(v_a)
    if s_merged is None:
        s_merged = torch.empty_like(s_a)
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.merge_state_v2.default(v_a, s_a, v_b, s_b, v_merged, s_merged)
    return v_merged, s_merged


def cutlass_mla_decode(
    q_nope: torch.Tensor,
    q_pe: torch.Tensor,
    kv_c_and_k_pe_cache: torch.Tensor,
    seq_lens: torch.Tensor,
    page_table: torch.Tensor,
    workspace: torch.Tensor,
    sm_scale: float,
    num_kv_splits: int = 1,  # Set to 1 to avoid cuda_graph issue by default.
) -> torch.Tensor:
    assert q_nope.ndim == 3, f"q_nope must be a 3D tensor, but got {q_nope.ndim}"
    assert q_pe.ndim == 3, f"q_pe must be a 3D tensor, but got {q_pe.ndim}"
    assert (
        kv_c_and_k_pe_cache.ndim == 3
    ), f"kv_c_and_k_pe_cache must be a 3D tensor, but got {kv_c_and_k_pe_cache.ndim}"

    B_q, H, D_q_nope = q_nope.shape
    B_q_2, H_2, D_q_pe = q_pe.shape
    assert (B_q == B_q_2) and (H == H_2)

    _, PAGE_SIZE, D_ckv = kv_c_and_k_pe_cache.shape

    D_latent = 512
    D_rope = 64
    assert D_q_nope == D_latent
    assert D_q_pe == D_rope
    assert D_ckv == D_latent + D_rope

    MAX_HEADS = 128
    assert H <= MAX_HEADS, f"H must be <= {MAX_HEADS}, but got {H}"
    if H < MAX_HEADS:
        q_nope_padded = q_nope.new_empty((B_q, MAX_HEADS, D_q_nope))
        q_nope_padded[:, :H] = q_nope
        q_nope = q_nope_padded

        q_pe_padded = q_pe.new_empty((B_q, MAX_HEADS, D_q_pe))
        q_pe_padded[:, :H] = q_pe
        q_pe = q_pe_padded

    assert len(page_table.shape) == 2
    B_block_table, block_num = page_table.shape
    assert B_block_table == B_q
    assert block_num > 0, f"block num must be greater than 0, got {block_num}"
    assert block_num % (128 / PAGE_SIZE) == 0

    # TODO(kaixih@nvidia): support fp8
    assert q_nope.dtype in (
        torch.float16,
        torch.bfloat16,
    ), f"q_nope.dtype needs to be fp16 or bf16 but got {q_nope.dtype}."
    assert q_nope.dtype == q_pe.dtype == kv_c_and_k_pe_cache.dtype
    assert (
        seq_lens.dtype == torch.int32
    ), f"seq_lens.dtype needs to be int32 but got {seq_lens.dtype}."
    assert (
        page_table.dtype == torch.int32
    ), f"page_table.dtype needs to be int32 but got {page_table.dtype}."

    out = q_nope.new_empty((B_q, MAX_HEADS, D_latent))

    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.cutlass_mla_decode.default(
        out,
        q_nope,
        q_pe,
        kv_c_and_k_pe_cache,
        seq_lens,
        page_table,
        workspace,
        sm_scale,
        num_kv_splits,
    )
    return out[:, :H].contiguous()


def cutlass_mla_get_workspace_size(
    max_seq_len: int,
    num_batches: int,
    sm_count: int = 0,
    num_kv_splits: int = 1,  # Set to 1 to avoid cuda_graph issue by default.
) -> int:
    assert max_seq_len > 0, f"max_seq_len must be greater than 0, got {max_seq_len}"
    assert num_batches > 0, f"num_batches must be greater than 0, got {num_batches}"
    return torch.ops.sgl_kernel.cutlass_mla_get_workspace_size.default(
        max_seq_len, num_batches, sm_count, num_kv_splits
    )

import pytest
import torch

from sglang.srt.utils import is_musa

if is_musa():
    import torchada  # noqa: F401


# This fixture ensures the torch defaults don't get left in modified states between
# tests (e.g., when a test fails before restoring the original value), which
# can cause subsequent tests to fail.
@pytest.fixture(autouse=True)
def reset_torch_defaults():
    orig_default_device = torch.get_default_device()
    orig_default_dtype = torch.get_default_dtype()
    yield
    torch.set_default_dtype(orig_default_dtype)
    torch.set_default_device(orig_default_device)

234	#define CHECK_CUDA_SUCCESS(cmd)                                         \
235	  do {                                                                  \
236	    cudaError_t e = cmd;                                                \
237	    if (e != cudaSuccess) {                                             \
238	      std::stringstream _message;                                       \
239	      auto s = cudaGetErrorString(e);                                   \
240	      _message << std::string(s) + "\n" << __FILE__ << ':' << __LINE__; \
241	      throw cuda_error(_message.str());                                 \
242	    }                                                                   \
243	  } while (0)

251	inline int getSMVersion() {
252	  int device{-1};
253	  CHECK_CUDA_SUCCESS(cudaGetDevice(&device));
254	  int sm_major = 0;
255	  int sm_minor = 0;
256	  CHECK_CUDA_SUCCESS(cudaDeviceGetAttribute(&sm_major, cudaDevAttrComputeCapabilityMajor, device));
257	  CHECK_CUDA_SUCCESS(cudaDeviceGetAttribute(&sm_minor, cudaDevAttrComputeCapabilityMinor, device));
258	  return sm_major * 10 + sm_minor;
259	}
260	
261	inline bool isDeviceType(const std::string& device_type) {
262	  int deviceCount;
263	  CHECK_CUDA_SUCCESS(cudaGetDeviceCount(&deviceCount));
264	
265	  int device_id = -1;
266	  if (deviceCount >= 1) {
267	    CHECK_CUDA_SUCCESS(cudaGetDevice(&device_id));
268	  } else {
269	    return false;
270	  }
271	
272	  cudaDeviceProp prop;
273	  CHECK_CUDA_SUCCESS(cudaGetDeviceProperties(&prop, device_id));
274	  if (device_type == std::string(prop.name)) {
275	    return true;
276	  }
277	  return false;
278	}

280	inline bool getBoolEnv(char const* name) {
281	  char const* env = std::getenv(name);
282	  return env && env[0] == '1' && env[1] == '\0';
283	}

285	inline bool getEnvEnablePDL() {
286	  static std::once_flag flag;
287	  static bool enablePDL = false;
288	  std::call_once(flag, [&]() {
289	    if (getSMVersion() >= 90) {
290	      // PDL will be enabled by setting the env variables `TRTLLM_ENABLE_PDL` to `1`
291	      enablePDL = getBoolEnv("TRTLLM_ENABLE_PDL");
292	    }
293	  });
294	  return enablePDL;
295	}

392	#define FULL_MASK 0xffffffff
393	
394	__device__ __forceinline__ float atomicMaxFloat(float* addr, float value) {
395	#ifndef USE_ROCM
396	  float old;
397	  old = (value >= 0) ? __int_as_float(atomicMax((int*)addr, __float_as_int(value)))
398	                     : __uint_as_float(atomicMin((unsigned int*)addr, __float_as_uint(value)));
399	  return old;
400	#else
401	  int* addr_as_i = (int*)addr;
402	  int old = *addr_as_i, assumed;
403	  do {
404	    assumed = old;
405	    old = atomicCAS(addr_as_i, assumed, __float_as_int(fmaxf(value, __int_as_float(assumed))));
406	  } while (assumed != old);
407	  return __int_as_float(old);
408	#endif
409	}
410	
411	__device__ __forceinline__ float warpReduceMax(float value) {
412	  value = fmaxf(value, __shfl_xor_sync(FULL_MASK, value, 16));
413	  value = fmaxf(value, __shfl_xor_sync(FULL_MASK, value, 8));
414	  value = fmaxf(value, __shfl_xor_sync(FULL_MASK, value, 4));
415	  value = fmaxf(value, __shfl_xor_sync(FULL_MASK, value, 2));
416	  value = fmaxf(value, __shfl_xor_sync(FULL_MASK, value, 1));
417	  return value;
418	}
419	
420	__device__ __forceinline__ float blockReduceMax(float value) {
421	  static __shared__ float warpLevelMaxs[WARP_SIZE];
422	  const int laneId = threadIdx.x % WARP_SIZE;
423	  const int warpId = threadIdx.x / WARP_SIZE;
424	
425	  value = warpReduceMax(value);
426	
427	  if (laneId == 0) warpLevelMaxs[warpId] = value;
428	  __syncthreads();
429	
430	  value = (threadIdx.x < blockDim.x / WARP_SIZE) ? warpLevelMaxs[laneId] : 0;
431	  if (warpId == 0) value = warpReduceMax(value);
432	
433	  return value;
434	}

436	// Pads to a multiple of `alignment` rows.
437	inline torch::Tensor pad_tensor(const torch::Tensor& tensor, int64_t alignment = 4, bool is_column_major = false) {
438	  int64_t rows = tensor.size(0);
439	  int64_t cols = tensor.size(1);
440	  int64_t pad_rows = (alignment - (rows % alignment)) % alignment;  // Compute padding size
441	
442	  if (pad_rows == 0) {
443	    return tensor;  // Already aligned
444	  }
445	
446	  torch::Tensor padding = torch::zeros({pad_rows, cols}, tensor.options());
447	  torch::Tensor tensor_padded = torch::cat({tensor, padding}, 0);  // Pad along rows
448	
449	  // Ensure column-major layout
450	  if (is_column_major) {
451	    return tensor_padded.t().contiguous().t();
452	  }
453	  return tensor_padded;
454	}
455	
456	// Get the next power of 2 of a number
457	inline uint32_t next_pow2(uint32_t x) noexcept {
458	  if (x <= 1) return 1;
459	  return 1u << (32 - __builtin_clz(x - 1));
460	}

from typing import Optional

import torch
from sgl_kernel.utils import _get_cache_buf


def awq_dequantize(
    qweight: torch.Tensor, scales: torch.Tensor, qzeros: torch.Tensor
) -> torch.ByteTensor:
    return torch.ops.sgl_kernel.awq_dequantize.default(qweight, scales, qzeros)


def int8_scaled_mm(mat_a, mat_b, scales_a, scales_b, out_dtype, bias=None):
    return torch.ops.sgl_kernel.int8_scaled_mm.default(
        mat_a,
        mat_b,
        scales_a,
        scales_b,
        out_dtype,
        bias,
    )


def fp8_scaled_mm(mat_a, mat_b, scales_a, scales_b, out_dtype, bias=None):
    return torch.ops.sgl_kernel.fp8_scaled_mm.default(
        mat_a,
        mat_b,
        scales_a,
        scales_b,
        out_dtype,
        bias,
    )


def _bmm_fp8_internal(
    workspace_buffer: torch.Tensor,
    A: torch.Tensor,
    B: torch.Tensor,
    D: torch.Tensor,
    A_scale: torch.Tensor,
    B_scale: torch.Tensor,
) -> None:
    cublas_handle = torch.cuda.current_blas_handle()
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.bmm_fp8.default(
        A,
        B,
        D,
        A_scale,
        B_scale,
        workspace_buffer,
        cublas_handle,
    )


def bmm_fp8(
    A: torch.Tensor,
    B: torch.Tensor,
    A_scale: torch.Tensor,
    B_scale: torch.Tensor,
    dtype: torch.dtype,
    out: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if out is None:
        out = torch.empty(
            (A.shape[0], A.shape[1], B.shape[2]),
            device=A.device,
            dtype=dtype,
        )
    workspace_buffer = _get_cache_buf("bmm_fp8_workspace", 32 * 1024 * 1024, A.device)
    _bmm_fp8_internal(workspace_buffer, A, B, out, A_scale, B_scale)
    return out


def dsv3_fused_a_gemm(
    mat_a: torch.Tensor,
    mat_b: torch.Tensor,
    output: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None,
) -> torch.Tensor:
    if output is None:
        output = torch.empty(
            (mat_a.shape[0], mat_b.shape[1]),
            device=mat_a.device,
            dtype=mat_a.dtype,
        )
    torch.ops.sgl_kernel.dsv3_fused_a_gemm.default(output, mat_a, mat_b)
    return output

    input: torch.Tensor,
